                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.problem_diagnosis.v1</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <template_id>
                            <value>EPS Patient Summary</value>
                        </template_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Problem/Diagnosis</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about a single identified health condition, injury, disability or any other issue which impacts on the physical, mental and/or social well-being of an individual.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Clear delineation between the scope of a problem versus a diagnosis is not easy to achieve in practice. For the purposes of clinical documentation with this archetype, problem and diagnosis are regarded as a continuum, with increasing levels of detail and supportive evidence usually providing weight towards the label of 'diagnosis'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">structure</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Problem/Diagnosis name</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the problem or diagnosis, by name.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the name of the problem or diagnosis with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Date/time clinically recognised</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimated or actual date/time the diagnosis or problem was recognised by a healthcare professional.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Partial dates are acceptable. If the subject of care is under the age of one year, then the complete date or a minimum of the month and year is necessary to enable accurate age calculations - for example, if used to drive decision support. Data captured/imported as "Age at time of clinical recognition" should be converted to a date using the subject's date of birth.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Severity</items>
                            <items id="description">An assessment of the overall severity of the problem or diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">If severity is included in the Problem/diagnosis name via precoordinated codes, this data element becomes redundant. Note: more specific grading of severity can be recorded using the Specific details SLOT.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">Clinical description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the problem or diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use to provide background and context, including evolution, episodes or exacerbations, progress and any other relevant details, about the problem or diagnosis.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                            <items id="text">Body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of a simple body site for the location of the problem or diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the name of the anatomical location with a terminology is preferred, where possible.
Use this data element to record precoordinated anatomical locations. If the requirements for recording the anatomical location are determined at run-time by the application or require more complex modelling such as relative locations then use the CLUSTER.anatomical_location or CLUSTER.relative_location within the 'Structured anatomical location' SLOT in this archetype. Occurrences for this data element are unbounded to allow for clinical scenarios such as describing a rash in multiple locations but where all of the other attributes are identical. If the anatomical location is included in the Problem/diagnosis name via precoordinated codes, this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0030">
                            <items id="text">Date/time of resolution</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimated or actual date/time of resolution or remission for this problem or diagnosis, as determined by a healthcare professional.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Partial dates are acceptable. If the subject of care is under the age of one year, then the complete date or a minimum of the month and year is necessary to enable accurate age calculations - for example, if used to drive decision support. Data captured/imported as "Age at time of resolution" should be converted to a date using the subject's date of birth.
</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0032">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0039">
                            <items id="text">Structured body site</items>
                            <items id="description">A structured anatomical location for the problem or diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this SLOT to insert the CLUSTER.anatomical_location or CLUSTER.relative_location archetypes if the requirements for recording the anatomical location are determined at run-time by the application or require more complex modelling such as relative locations.

If the anatomical location is included in the Problem/diagnosis name via precoordinated codes, use of this SLOT becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0043">
                            <items id="text">Specific details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details that are additionally required to record as unique attributes of this problem or diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">May include structured detail about the grading or staging of the diagnosis; diagnostic criteria, classification criteria or formal severity assessments such as Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0046">
                            <items id="text">Status</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured details for location-, domain-, episode- or workflow-specific aspects of the diagnostic process.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use status or context qualifiers with care, as they are variably used in practice and interoperability cannot be assured unless usage is clearly defined with the community of use. For example: active status - active, inactive, resolved, in remission; evolution status - initial, interim/working, final; temporal status - current, past; episodicity status - first, new, ongoing; admission status - admission, discharge; or priority status - primary, secondary.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0047">
                            <items id="text">Mild</items>
                            <items id="description">The problem or diagnosis does not interfere with normal activity or may cause damage to health if left untreated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0048">
                            <items id="text">Moderate</items>
                            <items id="description">The problem or diagnosis causes interference with normal activity or will damage health if left untreated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0049">
                            <items id="text">Severe</items>
                            <items id="description">The problem or diagnosis prevents normal activity or will seriously damage health if left untreated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0069">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the problem or diagnosis not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0070">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date this problem or diagnosis was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0071">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0072">
                            <items id="text">Course description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the course of the problem or diagnosis since onset.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0073">
                            <items id="text">Diagnostic certainty</items>
                            <items id="description">The level of confidence in the identification of the diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">If an alternative valueset is required, these values can be added to the DV_TEXT data type in a template.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0074">
                            <items id="text">Suspected</items>
                            <items id="description">The diagnosis has been identified with a low level of certainty.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0075">
                            <items id="text">Probable</items>
                            <items id="description">The diagnosis has been identified with a high level of certainty.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0076">
                            <items id="text">Confirmed</items>
                            <items id="description">The diagnosis has been confirmed against recognised criteria.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0077">
                            <items id="text">Date/time of onset</items>
                            <items id="description">Estimated or actual date/time that signs or symptoms of the problem/diagnosis were first observed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Data captured/imported as "Age at onset" should be converted to a date using the subject's date of birth.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0078">
                            <items id="text">Cause</items>
                            <items id="description">A cause, set of causes, or manner of causation of the problem or diagnosis.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Also known as 'aetiology' or 'etiology'. Coding with an external terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0079">
                            <items id="text">Variant</items>
                            <items id="description">Specific variant or subtype of the Diagnosis, if relevant.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'acute motor axonal neuropathy' as a variant of Guillain-Barre Syndrome. Coding of the name of the variant with a terminology is preferred, where possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0080">
                            <items id="text">Impact</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall assessment of the impact of a health problem for the specific patient.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The same diagnosis, with the same severity, has a different impact on each patient. This relevance qualifier implies adjusting the overall impact of that health problem according to the patient's characteristics.
For example: Two patients have the same diagnosis. First-degree burn.
One is a healthy person, the other is a diabetic patient.
The impact for that problem in a diabetic patient is HIGHER (more risk of infection..., longuer time healing).
This element is meant to allow for multiple types of impact, including but not limited to: clinical treatment, home functioning, work life.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>
                    <cardinality>

                        <is_unique>false</is_unique>

                            <lower_unbounded>false</lower_unbounded>
                            <upper_unbounded>true</upper_unbounded>
                            <lower>1</lower>
                        </interval>
                    </cardinality>

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.absence.v2</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Absence of information</items>
                            <items id="description">Statement that specified health information is not available for inclusion in the health record or extract at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Absence statement</items>
                            <items id="description">Positive statement that no information is available.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: "No information available about adverse reactions"; No information available about problems or diagnoses"; "No information available about previous procedures performed"; or "No information available about medications used".</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date at which the absence was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Reason for absence</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the reason why there is no information available.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: patient is unconscious or refuses to provide information. Coding the reason with a terminology is desirable, if possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                                                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                                            <items id="text">FHIR Narrative</items>
                                                            <items id="description">This cluster is intended to support the recording of FHIR narrative ingested that does not have a more natural target within existing archetypes. </items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                                            <items id="text">Status</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a human authored it and it may contain additional data.

</items>
                                                            <items id="comment">FHIR Codesystem: generated | extensions | additional | empty</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>
                                                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                                            <items id="text">Narrative</items>
                                                            <items id="description">The narrative carried in the div element in FHIR</items>
                                                        </term_definitions>

                                                <archetype_id>
                                                    <value>openEHR-EHR-CLUSTER.device.v1</value>
                                                </archetype_id>
                                                <template_id>
                                                    <value>EPS Patient Summary</value>
                                                </template_id>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                                    <items id="text">Medical device</items>
                                                    <items id="description">An instrument, apparatus, implant, material or similar, used in the provision of healthcare. In this context, a medical device includes a broad range of devices which act through a variety of physical, mechanical, thermal or similar means but specifically excludes devices which act through medicinal means such as pharmacological, metabolic or immunological methods.  The scope is inclusive of disposable devices as well as durable or persisting devices that require tracking, maintenance activities or regular calibration, recognising that each type of device has specific data recording requirements.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                                    <items id="text">Device name</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.
</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                                    <items id="text">Description</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Narrative description of the medical device.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                                    <items id="text">Type</items>
                                                    <items id="description">The category or kind of device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">Not applicable if a category is already recorded in 'Device name'.
 Example: if the 'Device' is named as a 'urinary catheter'; the 'Type' may be recorded as 'indwelling' or 'condom'.Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible. This may include use of GTIN or EAN numbers.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0004">
                                                    <items id="text">Manufacturer</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Name of manufacturer.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                                    <items id="text">Date of manufacture</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Date the device was manufactured.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0006">
                                                    <items id="text">Batch/Lot number</items>
                                                    <items id="description">The number assigned by the manufacturer which identifies a group of items manufactured at the same time, usually found on the label or packaging material.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                                    <items id="text">Date of expiry</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Date after which the device/product is no longer fit for use, usually found on the device itself or printed on the accompanying packaging.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">This date usually applies only to single use or disposable devices.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0008">
                                                    <items id="text">Comment</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Additional narrative about the device not captured in other fields.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0009">
                                                    <items id="text">Properties</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Further details about specific properties about the medical device.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0018">
                                                    <items id="text">Components</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Additional structured informations about identified components of the device.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0019">
                                                    <items id="text">Asset management</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Further details about management and maintenance of the device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">For example: Owner, Contact details, Location, Network address, Date due for replacement, Calibration details etc.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0020">
                                                    <items id="text">Serial number</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Number assigned by the manufacturer which can be found on the device, and should be specific to each device., its label, or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0021">
                                                    <items id="text">Unique device identifier (UDI)</items>
                                                    <items id="description">A numeric or alphanumeric string that is associated with this device within a given system.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">Often fixed to the device as a barcode.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0022">
                                                    <items id="text">Catalogue number</items>
                                                    <items id="description">The exact number assigned by the manufacturer, as it appears in the manufacturer's catalogue, device labeling, or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0023">
                                                    <items id="text">Model number</items>
                                                    <items id="description">The exact model number assigned by the manufacturer and found on the device label or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0024">
                                                    <items id="text">Other identifier</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Unspecified identifier, which can be further specified in a template or at run time.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">Coding of the name of the identifier with a coding system is desirable, if available.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0025">
                                                    <items id="text">Software version</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Identification of the version of software being used in the medical device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">When the medical device is an actual software application, record the version of the software using this data element. When the medical device has multiple software applications embedded within it, record each software component in a separate CLUSTER archetype within the Components SLOT - either as a nested instance of another CLUSTER.device archetype or using a CLUSTER archetype designed specifically for recording software details (but not yet available at time of this archetype development).</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0026">
                                                    <items id="text">Extension</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                                <term_definitions code="at0027">
                                                    <items id="text">Multimedia</items>
                                                    <items id="description">Digital representation of the device.</items>
                                                    <items id="comment">For example: a technical diagram of a device, or a digital image.</items>
                                                </term_definitions>
                                            </children>

                                            <items id="description">This cluster is intended to support the recording of FHIR narrative ingested that does not have a more natural target within existing archetypes. </items>

                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                            <items id="text">Status</items>

</items>
                                            <items id="comment">FHIR Codesystem: generated | extensions | additional | empty</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0002">

                        <archetype_id>
                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.device_summary.v0</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <template_id>
                            <value>EPS Patient Summary</value>
                        </template_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Medical device summary</items>
                            <items id="description">An ongoing and persistent overview about medical devices that have been fitted or implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Status</items>
                            <items id="description">Assertion about the fitting or implanting of devices, as at the date 'Last updated'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Never</items>
                            <items id="description">The device type has never been fitted or implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Current</items>
                            <items id="description">The device type is currently fitted or implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Previous</items>
                            <items id="description">The device type has been fitted or implanted in the past.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Device name</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the specific device, by name.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Start date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date of fitting or implant of the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                            <items id="text">End date</items>
                            <items id="description">Date when the device stopped being used or was removed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Structured detail</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional structured detail about the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0012">
                            <items id="text">Body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the body site where the device is fitted/implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0013">
                            <items id="text">Structured body site</items>
                            <items id="description">A structured anatomical location of the body site where the device is fitted/implanted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0015">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the use of the fitted device type.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0016">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0017">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date this summary was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                            <items id="text">Next review due</items>
                            <items id="description">Date on which this device should be reviewed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                            <items id="text">Device</items>
                            <items id="description">Name of the type of medical device.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Assisted hearing devices, eyeglasses, contact lens, dental braces, dentures, orthotics or artificial limbs.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                            <items id="text">Multimedia</items>
                            <items id="description">Digital image, video or diagram about the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                            <items id="text">Device details</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about each device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                            <items id="text">URI to original data</items>
                            <items id="description">Link to the original data about the fitting or insertion.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Commonly, this will be an ACTION archetype that contains the details about the fitting or insertion of the device.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>

                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Exclusion - global</items>
                            <items id="description">An overall statement of exclusion about all Problems/diagnoses, Family history, Medications, Procedures, Adverse reactions or other clinical items that are either not currently present, or have not been present in the past.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of procedures</items>
                            <items id="description">An overall statement of exclusion about all Problems/diagnoses, Family history, Medications, Procedures, Adverse reactions or other clinical items.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'No previous diagnoses', 'No known family history', 'No known history of adverse reactions', 'Never taken any medications' or 'No known operations or significant procedures'.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of problems/diagnoses</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion of all problems or diagnoses at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of family history</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion of all significant health-related problems in relatives or family members of the individual at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of medication use</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about the use of all medications at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of procedures</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about all procedures at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Global exclusion of adverse reactions</items>
                            <items id="description">Overall statement of exclusion about all adverse reactions at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0010">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: Local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0011">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional comment not covered in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                            <value>openEHR-EHR-EVALUATION.absence.v2</value>
                        </archetype_id>
                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Absence of information</items>
                            <items id="description">Statement that specified health information is not available for inclusion in the health record or extract at the time of recording.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Absence statement</items>
                            <items id="description">Positive statement that no information is available.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: "No information available about adverse reactions"; No information available about problems or diagnoses"; "No information available about previous procedures performed"; or "No information available about medications used".</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Last updated</items>
                            <items id="description">The date at which the absence was last updated.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Reason for absence</items>
                            <items id="description">Description of the reason why there is no information available.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: patient is unconscious or refuses to provide information. Coding the reason with a terminology is desirable, if possible.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local content or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements or additional metadata to align with FHIR or CIMI equivalents.</items>
                        </term_definitions>

                                            <items id="description">This cluster is intended to support the recording of FHIR narrative ingested that does not have a more natural target within existing archetypes. </items>

                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">

</items>

                                        </term_definitions>

                                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                                            <items id="text">Focal device</items>
                                            <items id="description">An instrument, apparatus, implant, material or similar, used in the provision of healthcare. In this context, a medical device includes a broad range of devices which act through a variety of physical, mechanical, thermal or similar means but specifically excludes devices which act through medicinal means such as pharmacological, metabolic or immunological methods.  The scope is inclusive of disposable devices as well as durable or persisting devices that require tracking, maintenance activities or regular calibration, recognising that each type of device has specific data recording requirements.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                            <items id="text">Device name</items>
                                            <items id="description">Identification of the medical device, preferably by a common name, a formal fully descriptive name or, if required, by class or category of device.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">This data element will capture the term, phrase or category used in clinical practice. For example: <brand name><machine> (XYZ Audiometer); <size> <brand name> <intravenous catheter> (14G Jelco IV catheter); or <brand name/type> <implant>. Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible, although this may be local and depending on local supplies available.
</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                                            <items id="description">Narrative description of the medical device.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                                            <items id="text">Type</items>
                                            <items id="description">The category or kind of device.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">Not applicable if a category is already recorded in 'Device name'.
 Example: if the 'Device' is named as a 'urinary catheter'; the 'Type' may be recorded as 'indwelling' or 'condom'.Coding with a terminology is desirable, where possible. This may include use of GTIN or EAN numbers.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                                            <items id="text">Manufacturer</items>
                                            <items id="description">Name of manufacturer.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                                            <items id="text">Date of manufacture</items>
                                            <items id="description">Date the device was manufactured.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                                            <items id="text">Batch/Lot number</items>
                                            <items id="description">The number assigned by the manufacturer which identifies a group of items manufactured at the same time, usually found on the label or packaging material.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                                            <items id="text">Date of expiry</items>
                                            <items id="description">Date after which the device/product is no longer fit for use, usually found on the device itself or printed on the accompanying packaging.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">This date usually applies only to single use or disposable devices.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0008">
                                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the device not captured in other fields.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0009">
                                            <items id="text">Properties</items>
                                            <items id="description">Further details about specific properties about the medical device.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0018">
                                            <items id="text">Components</items>
                                            <items id="description">Additional structured informations about identified components of the device.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0019">
                                            <items id="text">Asset management</items>
                                            <items id="description">Further details about management and maintenance of the device.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">For example: Owner, Contact details, Location, Network address, Date due for replacement, Calibration details etc.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0020">
                                            <items id="text">Serial number</items>
                                            <items id="description">Number assigned by the manufacturer which can be found on the device, and should be specific to each device., its label, or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0021">
                                            <items id="text">Unique device identifier (UDI)</items>
                                            <items id="description">A numeric or alphanumeric string that is associated with this device within a given system.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">Often fixed to the device as a barcode.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0022">
                                            <items id="text">Catalogue number</items>
                                            <items id="description">The exact number assigned by the manufacturer, as it appears in the manufacturer's catalogue, device labeling, or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0023">
                                            <items id="text">Model number</items>
                                            <items id="description">The exact model number assigned by the manufacturer and found on the device label or accompanying packaging.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0024">
                                            <items id="text">Other identifier</items>
                                            <items id="description">Unspecified identifier, which can be further specified in a template or at run time.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">Coding of the name of the identifier with a coding system is desirable, if available.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                                            <items id="text">Software version</items>
                                            <items id="description">Identification of the version of software being used in the medical device.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">When the medical device is an actual software application, record the version of the software using this data element. When the medical device has multiple software applications embedded within it, record each software component in a separate CLUSTER archetype within the Components SLOT - either as a nested instance of another CLUSTER.device archetype or using a CLUSTER archetype designed specifically for recording software details (but not yet available at time of this archetype development).</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                                            <items id="text">Multimedia</items>
                                            <items id="description">Digital representation of the device.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">For example: a technical diagram of a device, or a digital image.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                    </children>

                                            <items id="comment">Coding of the name of the identifier with a coding system is desirable, if available.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0025">
                                            <items id="text">Software version</items>
                                            <items id="description">Identification of the version of software being used in the medical device.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">When the medical device is an actual software application, record the version of the software using this data element. When the medical device has multiple software applications embedded within it, record each software component in a separate CLUSTER archetype within the Components SLOT - either as a nested instance of another CLUSTER.device archetype or using a CLUSTER archetype designed specifically for recording software details (but not yet available at time of this archetype development).</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0026">
                                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                                            <items id="description">Additional information required to capture local context or to align with other reference models/formalisms.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>
                                        <term_definitions code="at0027">
                                            <items id="text">Multimedia</items>
                                            <items id="description">Digital representation of the device.</items>
                                            <items id="comment">For example: a technical diagram of a device, or a digital image.</items>
                                        </term_definitions>

                                            <items id="description">This cluster is intended to support the recording of FHIR narrative ingested that does not have a more natural target within existing archetypes. </items>

                                        <term_definitions code="at0001">
                                            <items id="text">Status</items>
                                            <items id="description">The status of the narrative - whether it's entirely generated (from just the defined data or the extensions too), or whether a human authored it and it may contain additional data.

</items>

                        <term_definitions code="at0000">
                            <items id="text">Procedure</items>
                            <items id="description">A clinical activity carried out for screening, investigative, diagnostic, curative, therapeutic, evaluative or palliative purposes.</items>

                            <items id="text">Tree</items>

                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0002">
                            <items id="text">Procedure name</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the procedure by name.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Procedure name' with a terminology is recommended, if available.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0003">
                            <items id="text">Procedure detail</items>
                            <items id="description">Structured information about the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use to capture detailed, structured information about anatomical location, method & technique, equipment used, devices implanted, results, findings etc.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0004">
                            <items id="text">Procedure planned</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure to be undertaken is planned.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0005">
                            <items id="text">Comment</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional narrative about the procedure or care pathway step not captured in other fields.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0006">
                            <items id="text">Complication</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about any complication arising from the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this data element to record simple terms or precoordinated complications. For example: Hematuria after a kidney biopsy, tissue irritation after insertion of intravenous catheter. If the requirements for recording complication are more complex then use of a specific CLUSTER archetype within the 'Procedure detail' SLOT in this archetype is advised and this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0007">
                            <items id="text">Procedure request sent</items>
                            <items id="description">Request for procedure sent.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0014">
                            <items id="text">Reason</items>
                            <items id="description">Reason that the activity or care pathway step for the identified procedure was carried out.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the reason for the cancellation or suspension of the procedure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0034">
                            <items id="text">X - Procedure planned</items>
                            <items id="description">This pathway step has been deprecated as it was incorrectly associated with 'initial' status - use the new 'Procedure planned' (at0004) pathway step  which is correctly associated with 'planned' status.</items>
                            <items id="comment">(Was: The procedure to be undertaken is planned.)</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0035">
                            <items id="text">X - Procedure request sent</items>
                            <items id="description">This pathway step has been deprecated as it was incorrectly associated with 'initial' status - use the new 'Procedure request sent' (at0007) pathway step  which is correctly associated with 'planned' status.</items>
                            <items id="comment">(Was: Request for procedure sent.)</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0036">
                            <items id="text">Procedure scheduled</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been scheduled.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0038">
                            <items id="text">Procedure postponed</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been postponed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0039">
                            <items id="text">Procedure cancelled</items>
                            <items id="description">The planned procedure has been cancelled prior to commencement.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0040">
                            <items id="text">Procedure suspended</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been suspended.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0041">
                            <items id="text">Procedure aborted</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been aborted.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0043">
                            <items id="text">Procedure completed</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure has been performed and all associated clinical activities completed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0047">
                            <items id="text">Procedure performed</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure, or subprocedure in a multicomponent procedure, has been performed.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0048">
                            <items id="text">Outcome</items>
                            <items id="description">Outcome of procedure performed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Outcome' with a terminology is recommended, if available.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0049">
                            <items id="text">Description</items>
                            <items id="description">Narrative description about the procedure, as appropriate for the pathway step.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: description about the performance and findings from the the procedure, the aborted attempt or the cancellation of the procedure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0053">
                            <items id="text">Tree</items>
                            <items id="description">@ internal @</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0054">
                            <items id="text">Requestor order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">The local ID assigned to the order by the healthcare provider or organisation requesting the service.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This is equivalent to Placer Order Number in HL7 v2 specifications.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0055">
                            <items id="text">Requestor</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the healthcare provider or organisation requesting the service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0056">
                            <items id="text">Receiver order identifier</items>
                            <items id="description">The ID assigned to the order by the healthcare provider or organisation receiving the request for service. This is also referred to as Filler Order Identifier.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This is equivalent to Filler Order Number in HL7 v2 specifications.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0057">
                            <items id="text">Receiver</items>
                            <items id="description">Details about the healthcare provider or organisation receiving the request for service.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0058">
                            <items id="text">Urgency</items>
                            <items id="description">Urgency of the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Coding of the 'Urgency' with a terminology is recommended, if available.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0060">
                            <items id="text">End datetime</items>
                            <items id="description">The date or datetime when the entire procedure, or the last component of a multicomponent procedure, was finished.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Only for use in association with the 'Procedure performed' pathway step, and in situations where the procedure is repeated on multiple occasions before being completed or there are multiple components to the whole procedure. This may be the same as the RM time attribute for the 'Procedure completed' pathway step.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0061">
                            <items id="text">Total duration</items>
                            <items id="description">The total amount of time taken to complete the procedure, which may include time spent during the active phase of the procedure plus time during which the procedure was suspended.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Only for use in association with the 'Procedure completed' pathway steps.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0062">
                            <items id="text">Multimedia</items>
                            <items id="description">Mulitimedia representation of a performed procedure.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0063">
                            <items id="text">Body site</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of the body site for the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Occurrences for this data element are unbounded to allow for clinical scenarios such as removing multiple skin lesions in different places, but where all of the other attributes are identical. Use this data element to record simple terms or precoordinated anatomical locations. If the requirements for recording the anatomical location are determined at run-time by the application or require more complex modelling such as relative locations then use the CLUSTER.anatomical_location or CLUSTER.relative_location within the 'Procedure detail' SLOT in this archetype. If the anatomical location is included in the 'Procedure name' via precoordinated codes, this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0064">
                            <items id="text">Extension</items>
                            <items id="description">Additional information required to extend the model with local content or to align with other reference models or formalisms.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: local information requirements; or additional metadata to align with FHIR.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0065">
                            <items id="text">Method</items>
                            <items id="description">Identification of specific method or technique for the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Use this data element to record simple terms or a narrative description. If the requirements for recording the method require more complex modelling then this can be represented by additional archetypes within the 'Procedure detail' SLOT in this archetype. If the method is included in the 'Procedure name' via precoordinated codes, this data element becomes redundant.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0066">
                            <items id="text">Scheduled datetime</items>
                            <items id="description">The date or datetime on which the procedure is intended to be performed.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Only for use in association with the 'Procedure scheduled' pathway step.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0067">
                            <items id="text">Procedure type</items>
                            <items id="description">The type of procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">This pragmatic data element may be used to support organisation within the user interface.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0068">
                            <items id="text">Procedure commenced</items>
                            <items id="description">The procedure, or subprocedure in a multicomponent procedure, has been commenced.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0069">
                            <items id="text">Procedural difficulty</items>
                            <items id="description">Difficulties or issues encountered during performance of the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">Examples: The patient was agitated, insufficient emptying of the stomach before gastroscopy, a tumour in the bile ducts made it impossible to pass the scope through.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0070">
                            <items id="text">Indication</items>
                            <items id="description">The clinical or process-related reason for the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: 'Failed bowel preparation' or 'Bowel cancer screening'. Coding of the 'Indication' with a terminology is recommended, if available.  This data element allows multiple occurrences to enable the user to record multiple responses, if required.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0071">
                            <items id="text">Impact</items>
                            <items id="description">Assessment of the impact of a procedure or its outcome for a specific patient.</items>
                            <items id="comment">The same procedure, with the same severity or outcome, can have a different impact on each patient. This relevance factor involves adjusting the overall impact of that procedure or outcome according to the patient's characteristics.

For example: Two patients have undergone the same procedure with the same outcome: osteosynthesis repair of the fifth finger of their non-dominant hand.

One is a gardener or construction worker; the other, a pianist.

The impact of that procedure on the pianist is GREATER (longer sick leave, need for full functional rehabilitation to adapt to the high fine motor demands of work).

This element is intended to allow for multiple types of impact, including but not limited to: clinical treatment, home functioning, or work life.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0072">
                            <items id="text">Intent</items>
                            <items id="description">The intended outcome of the procedure.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: An operation with curative intent may be planned, while during the operation it is discovered that curative treatment is not possible and the operation is carried out with a palliative intent instead. Coding of the 'Intent' with a terminology is recommended, if available. This data element allows multiple occurrences to enable the user to record multiple responses, if required.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0073">
                            <items id="text">Start datetime</items>
                            <items id="description">The date or datetime when the entire procedure, or the first component of a multicomponent procedure, was first started.</items>
                            <items id="comment">For example: the knife to skin time. Only for use in association with the 'Procedure performed', 'Procedure suspended', 'Procedure aborted' or 'Procedure completed' pathway steps. This may be the same as the RM 'time' attribute for the 'Procedure commenced' pathway step.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                        <term_definitions code="at0074">
                            <items id="text">Pathway step duration</items>
                            <items id="description">The duration of time between the start of the most recent pathway step for this procedure and the start of the current pathway step.</items>
                        </term_definitions>
                    </children>
                    <cardinality>
                        <is_ordered>false</is_ordered>
                        <is_unique>false</is_unique>

                            <lower_unbounded>false</lower_unbounded>
                            <upper_unbounded>true</upper_unbounded>
                            <lower>1</lower>
                        </interval>
                    </cardinality>
